code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip('"')
        # remove commas inside numbers for readability
        if o.replace(".", "", 1).isdigit():
            if o.endswith(".0"):
                o = o[:-2]
        return o

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    templates = {
        "cityServed": "{subject} serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subject} is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length at {subject} is {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "The runway at {subject} is named {obj}",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{subject} is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "{subject} is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO code of {subject} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "The location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA code of {subject} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "The first runway at {subject} is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "The first runway at {subject} is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway surface at {subject} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "The first runway number at {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "The runway surface at {subject} is {obj}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {obj}",
        "capital": "{subject} has capital {obj}",
        "language": "{subject} language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subject} official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subject} uses the {obj}",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "{subject} was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "{subject} was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subject} has postal code {obj}",
        "areaCode": "{subject} has area code {obj}",
        "largestCity": "{subject} has largest city {obj}",
        "countySeat": "{subject} has county seat {obj}",
        "mayor": "{subject} has mayor {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{subject} has leader party {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject} has leader title {obj}",
        "chief": "{subject} has chief {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "{subject} has hub airport {obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subject} serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "{subject} has native name {obj}",
        "class": "{subject} class is {obj}",
        "division": "{subject} division is {obj}",
        "order": "{subject} order is {obj}",
        "species": "{subject} species is {obj}",
        "demonym": "{subject} demonym is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subject} uses currency {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)
            if tmpl:
                # If the template already includes the subject, render it directly
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    sentences.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj) + ".")
                else:
                    parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine remaining parts (those without full‑sentence templates) into one sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all generated sentences; if there is only one, return it without extra spaces
    return " ".join(sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
